calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And To Enter Judgment In Conformance With The Award
Off Calendar Nov-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lisa Gibson, Esq. and Crispin Collins, Esq. 424-221-7405 of Nelson Mullins for Petitioner.
Gavin Hughes, Esq. 916-900-8022 of GMH for Respondent.

9:31 am. The Court hands out copies of its tentative ruling to the parties and gives them time to read.

10:00 am. The matter is recalled.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. Respondent Argues notice requirement, exclusive jurisdiction. Petitioner responds and accepts the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER SUBARU OF AMERICA, INC.'S Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And To Enter Judgment In Conformance With The Award.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

***After the hearing, an Order granting Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award and to Enter Judgment in Conformance with Award is signed and filed. (see scanned order for details)***

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And To Enter Judgment In Conformance With The Award
Off Calendar Nov-07-2019 Continued To Nov-15-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER SUBARU OF AMERICA, INC.'s Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And To Enter Judgment In Conformance With The Award.

Continued to 11/15/19 per stipulation of the parties and approval of the Court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Maurice Sanchez & Atty. Lisa M. Gibson of Nelson Mullins, Tel. No. 1-424-221- 7403 appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty, Gavin M. Hughes and Atty. Robert A. Mayville of the Law Offices of
Gavin M. Hughes, Tel. No. 1-925-457-2028 appearing for the Respondent.


RULING - A R G U E D; Petitioner Subaru of America, Inc.'s (SOA's) petition to compel arbitration is taken under submission. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/ru

* * * After hearing, the court issued an order as follows: Subaru of America, Inc.'s (SOA's) petition to compel arbitration is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Subaru Dealer Satellite Service Facility Agreement does not come within the exception to arbitration provided by the Motor Vehicle Franchise Contract Arbitration Fairness Act (MVFCAFA), because it is not a contract under which motor vehicles are both serviced and sold. Moreover, the service agreement is neither illegal nor unconscionable; both parties are sophisticates with bargaining power. On the other hand, the Putnam Subaru dealer agreement does fit the MVFCAFA exception, because it regards the sale and service of motor vehicles, and discontinuing the service agreement could conceivably be found to modify and/or less plausibly, terminate the dealer agreement. Thus, Putnam is compelled to arbitrate claims arising from the service agreement and SOA's petition is to that extent GRANTED. However, I DENY the part of SOA's prayer that would compel Putnam to dismiss its New Motor Vehicle Board protests, because discontinuing the service agreement might be found to modify the dealer agreement. I recognize that these rulings could lead to actions that are partially parallel, but they are what the law requires. The parties are encouraged to agree to stay either the arbitration or the Board proceedings.* * *

Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Continued To Jun-22-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONER SUBARU OF AMERICA, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is continued to 6-22-18 to be heard by Judge Ulmer. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 (302/HEK)
